title: the only video you'll ever need to escape 0 view jail (no bs)

transcript.text: Hey guys, today I'm going to be going over probably the most requested topic I've received and that's how to escape zero view jail. And this is probably one of the most frustrating topics because I really do think that every aspiring shorts creator faces this at some point. I've personally faced this like many times. One thing I want you guys to understand is that I'm actually really talking about any short that gets anywhere from zero to even like 200 views. If you check the analytics of your shorts and you find out that most of your views, like 99% of your views are not coming from your from the shorts feed, that's cooked. That's zero view jail. And usually that'll result in maybe a few views from like YouTube search or like suggested videos. But like the reason that shorts get views is because they get pushed into the shorts feed, meaning this thing. So if they don't get pushed into here, like you're not going to get views on them, bro. So if you're getting that low of views, what's happening usually is just YouTube is just straight up not pushing your video to the algorithm. even a super bad shorts video. If YouTube pushes it, it'll get a few thousand views at least. So, my goal with this video is to try to create the greatest guide to zero view jail that I possibly can make. I want this to be the last video you'll ever hopefully have to watch on this topic. And I do want to say this first, I'm going to try to be as in-depth as I possibly can because there isn't a silver bullet that just fixes this problem. I want to be as honest and transparent with you guys as possible. This is probably going to be an extremely frustrating process because of the fact that there just isn't a simple fix. And there's so many things that could be causing this that are in and out of your control. But if you truly want to succeed in YouTube shorts, you just have to remain dedicated and plug away each day until you finally get a breakthrough. Like I said, I know it's frustrating. I know it feels unfair, but that's just the way the world works, guys. It's stupid. It shouldn't exist, but all hope isn't lost because usually there is at least some way around this. But for most people, it is going to require a lot of troubleshooting. So, just be ready and prepared for that. So, what is the zero view jail exactly? For whatever reason, YouTube thinks you're a bot or is shadowbanning you. What you guys need to understand is that this isn't about your content being bad or good. This is just straight up YouTube is not giving you a chance. I have had channels before that were stuck in zero view jail and then when I looked online, everybody was saying, "Oh, bro, just upload better content. Shadowbs aren't real." Then I upload them on a different channel, the exact same video. Boom. The video gets like 500,000 views. Yes, shadow bands are absolutely real. And don't listen to anybody who says they aren't. I'm going to start with some basics here, and it'll probably get more advanced as the video goes on. I want to make this video as concise as possible, but to be honest, guys, there's just so much information here that this is one of the most complicated topics when it comes to shorts. So, I don't know how long this video will be exactly. We'll see. If you guys have questions, before you type them in the comments, please watch the video all the way through because it might be answered later on in the video. Like I said, I'm going to try to make this as in-depth as I can. This video is probably going to be all over the place, but if there's one thing I can guarantee, it'll be the most comprehensive, greatest video on this topic that you'll ever see. I think that's mostly everything I need to talk about before we get into it. So, let's start this. Okay, so like I said, starting out with the basic here, conventional knowledge is to use an aged account. And for the sake of this video, I would say an aged account is anything that's like at least 6 months old, but preferably one or two years. It's possible to get pushed into the algorithm right away if your account's new, but it's harder. Now, on this channel, for example, it did end up getting pushed, my Money Boy Max channel, but that's because I'm uploading all long- form content. I have no shorts. So, like, I wouldn't have joined March 31st and just uploaded my first shorts. Generally, I would say if you're going to make a new account, I personally do suggest a quote unquote warm-up period of a few days at least. And in this period, here's what you're going to be doing. So, you're going to use the account like a regular person would. You're not going to have videos playing all day like one of these 10-hour long e-commerce courses. You also don't want to like every single video. Like I like this one here. God, this dude big. This guy looks like Dave Blunts if you know him or like Badlands Chugs. Anyways, you don't want to like every single video. What I like to do is pick out a few random interesting videos I'd watch anyways on my main account and watch them on the new channel. Interact with it like normal. Don't like every single video, but if you like the creator, like and subscribe. Interact with the comments. So go in here and like like different comments and then respond comment to other people, but make sure you aren't typing out anything that could be easily constructed by a bot. Like just please talk like a normal human. I know it's hard for a lot of you guys. Don't spam emojis or just all caps lock comments, please. But there's generally not really a formula here. There's not like, oh, you watch an hour 7 days straight and you'll get out of YouTube jail. Just use the account to watch and interact with like two to three long form videos a day. Scroll in the shorts feed for a bit. Same concept there with interacting with the content and the people in the comments. Do that for two to three days, a few hours maximum for each day. And then you're going to try to post for the first time after that. If it's stuck in the zero of due jail, try warming it up for a few more days and then try posting again. The next thing is that you're going to go to YouTube Studio on the account you're wanting to upload on. Make sure this is a laptop or computer and you're going to click the settings button in the bottom left corner and then you're going to select channel. I usually select the country, so just do United States. But then you're also going to go to feature eligibility here. And what you're going to do is you're going to make sure each of these steps are completed and then you're going to save the changes. Now, don't just complete these uh verification steps and then instantly start posting. Make sure these are completed first thing and for a few days before you upload. They'll usually want you to do a phone number verification and then like a video verification with your face in it. Um and you can just do that with your phone. Don't worry, nobody's going to see your goofy a face. It's all automated and done by robots. Now, you can only verify uh two channels per year with the same phone number. So, if you've already done that, you should be able to get a free phone number by using Google Voice, or you can use a friend or a family member's phone number and have them give you the verification code. Just make sure it's not somebody who you'd ever have a fallout with. So, like your mom or dad would be good options if you have a non-abusive family. If you've already done these things and tried with multiple accounts, check to see if they're all under the same email. And you can either make a new email or try a different email account. Some email accounts are just legitimately boned. I have no idea why, but like no matter what you do, they will just not work. Like I said, I know a lot of this is going to just be frustrating troubleshooting, but if you can find a good email address that works for you, you can usually create a few different channels. And with a proper warm-up, sometimes any account on that email will get pushed, which will completely avoid zero view jail. For example, all of my successful accounts really come from like one email, and I have like five, bro. Now, when you start to upload, if you're just starting to upload, I would only upload once per day. On my first channel, if I ever uploaded within the same like 10-hour period, the second video just wouldn't get pushed. Even today, I mostly only do one short per day on all of my channels. Although, once your channel blows up, most of the time you can upload more. On my main channel, until I got like 10,000 subs, I swear every once in a while, my videos would just not get pushed. So, it would be like 15K views, 15K views, 15K views, 15K views, 100 views, 50 views, back to 15K views. It was like the dumbest thing ever. In most cases, if the video isn't getting pushed to the shorts feed after 1 hour and you already have other videos getting pushed, I personally unlist the video. Now, some people say it works for them when they private it. But the most important thing is that you just do not delete it. Only unlist it or private it. And just a side note here, the only reason that I only wait an hour is just because the channel already has been pushed before. If it's a new channel, wait at least 24 to 48 hours to see if you're really stuck in zero view jail. cuz sometimes the algorithm is just weird and it's like these videos will get pushed in like a day or two. But anyways, you unlist it or private it, then wait a day and upload it again with different captions, a different title, and also when you upload it, upload it on your phone in YouTube, and you're going to cut off like 0.1 or 2 seconds at the end of the video. So, this will change the metadata of the video so YouTube doesn't think you're spamming the same video. And this is super important. If you're going to upload the same video that got zero views to a different channel, don't just re-upload it exactly, guys. Change the video time slightly. Change the cap cut captions, the font. Just change it up a bit. It doesn't need to be major. You can change the title, description, tags, etc. There is so many things you can change that will help out your video. Don't delete videos, guys. Please, especially deleting a lot of videos, private or unlist every single time. I want to stress this too, like something important is that YouTube does do this randomly sometimes when your channel is still growing. So, if like two or three videos don't get pushed, it's not the end of the world. Keep uploading. You never know, your next video might uh get pushed again. Now, I know this is also going to sound schizophrenic, and it's different for everybody, but I swear sometimes when I post shorts that contain women or kids, even though they are completely harmless, YouTube completely shadowbans those videos. For me, I never figured out a way around that. I don't know if YouTube just thinks I'm like a predator or something. Once my channel went viral, I was able to start posting videos of women and kids, but until like 10,000 subs, bro, those videos were not getting pushed for me. Now, on one of the channels I tried to start, I was stuck in zero view jail, even though the video was fire. And I did the unlisting trick, and it didn't work. I waited a day, did the unlisting trick again, and when I posted it on the second day, I used my phone's data instead of Wi-Fi. And when I didn't use my Wi-Fi, the video got pushed. Now, on that channel, whenever I had upload with Wi-Fi, got zero views. I uploaded with mobile data for 30 days, and every single video got pushed. Then once the channel got a few thousand subscribers, I tried Wi-Fi again and now I can post with Wi-Fi on that channel and have it be pushed. Maybe that will work for you, maybe not. Maybe it was just a coincidence or placebo, but these are all things you should try. Now, the next tip is from Money Mind Unfiltered. I'm sure most of you guys probably know him. On the channel that's stuck in zero view jails, upload a long form video with your face in it just talking about something random. Leave it up for a day or two, then unlist that video and try to post shorts again. Anecdotally, something you guys might find interesting is that most of my successful channels that got pushed, I actually was uploading long form before I repurposed those channels. And the long form videos I uploaded before didn't even have my face in them. It was just me playing games or something. If you don't use an account for like 5 years, I've never had success posting on an account like that, even if it had previous activity and was aged. Now, I'm not saying it can't work for you, but I think if you're extremely inactive on a YouTube channel for years or even months, and then you come back and start suddenly uploading videos, even though the account is aged, it's never really worked for me. Definitely. Definitely for those super old channels you haven't used in years, I would say you still need to warm them up. Don't think just because it's an aged account, it'll work out. Now, copyright strikes, not copyright claims, but copyright strikes, they would show up around here in your dashboard. They have always killed any reach I had until they were either resolved or until exactly 2 weeks from the strike being issued. In my personal experience, literally the one time I had a copyright strike, it was at like 3 p.m. and I lost all of my views right then and there. Dropped off a cliff. And I swear to God, like 14 days later at exactly 3:00 p.m., it's like my view faucet just turned back on. It was insane. Another thing, scheduling shorts has never pushed it to the algorithm for me. I don't know why. I know other people do it and it works fine for them. It'll be different for everybody, but it always put me in zero view jail and not getting pushed to the shorts feed every single time. I would upload normally if you can. Maybe that's just a me thing. Now, make sure when you upload a video, you select no, this content is not made for kids, unless it is specifically marketed for like toddlers. And also, you want to check no, this video does not contain altered content, unless you're literally posting AI videos, in which case you do want to hit that. Also, when you upload a video or just on your channel, in the settings, go to category and change this to whatever category you are posting in. If it's like fitness, for example, it's just people and blogs. If it's gaming, it's a gaming right here. You're going to want to be careful about drastically and randomly switching up the type of content you're posting. Sometimes this can confuse the algorithm. You can post different videos. Like, don't be scared, but if you change your channel from like military videos to like celebrities, the algorithm might be confused for a bit. You're also going to want to make sure that the content you're posting isn't just re-uploads of other people's videos. Do not just go on to Tik Tok, download a video like this. You want to make sure you're editing the videos or tweaking them a bit. If the videos are just ripped and re-uploaded, nine times out of 10, they won't get pushed. You need to add something new. YouTube especially shadowbans channels that do this. That's why, for like example, there are channels that literally just re-upload a video from FitFix and they usually get shadowbanned even though the original video has like millions of views. Because even though the video is highquality and can get a lot of views when FitFix posted it, YouTube detects you're using unoriginal content and flags that video and your channel. So then when we go to his channel, notice how pretty much nothing is getting pushed. If you've been uploading for a while and got pushed and then suddenly you're stuck in zero view jail, it could be a few things. Number one, start uploading every other day instead of once or twice every day. Number two, you're going to try to upload from a different device. Like if you usually upload from your phone, try uploading from your computer and vice versa. Number three, if you're uploading on your computer, there should be an option that says send this video first to your subscribers or something. And you're going to want to uncheck that box. It should be in like the advanced settings when you're uploading a video. So, just uncheck show your subscribers first and try to upload it again. Another thing you can do is test out whether the shorts hashtag in your title has any effect. So, instead of the title just being like this guy did something crazy, it'll be this guy did something crazy # shorts. Adding on to that, you can also try to upload from within the YouTube Studio app on your phone instead of from the YouTube app. The time you upload doesn't really matter that much, but just try to upload around the same time every time you post. For example, if you're posting every day around 6:00 p.m., you can post at like 7:00 p.m. or 5:30 p.m. But I wouldn't just randomly post at 8:00 a.m. one day. Also, don't use the same title on shorts that you post every day. YouTube might mark it as spam. So, for example, so like for example, for every video, don't make the title like don't click the sound or something. It can work for some people, but if you're getting zero views and doing that, cut it out, bro. If you've had a video up for a few days with zero views, before you unlist it, you could always try to change the title, add shorts in the title, like I said, change the description a little bit. I have had a video that was stuck on zero views before I changed the title, and immediately it started getting views. Now, if you've tried everything and have been consistently uploading once per day or once every other day for two weeks while using the account and interacting with other people's content and none of the videos have been pushed to the shorts feed, I would start a new account or a new Gmail account and restart this whole process until you find a YouTube account that works. If you do all of these steps every single time you start a channel and try every single thing I laid out in this video, I can almost guarantee that you will eventually get a channel that will be pushed to the shorts feed. I'm sure there's other things. If I learn anything new about new ways to get out of the zero view jail, I'll upload a video letting you guys know. If you think this video was valuable, guys, please like and subscribe because this took a lot of work for me to make. But I do hope this helps some of you guys. Peace.
